pageTitle: Privacy and personal data processing document
pageSubtitle: Personal data processing policy in the employment context
intro:
This document describes the processing of your personal data in the context of your employment relationship with your employer, in compliance with applicable personal data protection regulations in the Americas, including: Law 21.719 (Chile), the Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados - LGPD (Brazil), Law 25.326 (Argentina), the Federal Law on Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties - LFPDPPP (Mexico), Law 1581 of 2012 (Colombia), Law 29733 (Peru), Law 18.331 (Uruguay), and other applicable provisions depending on your country of residence or place of work.

This document aims to inform you clearly, transparently and comprehensively about who processes your data, for what purposes, what categories of data are processed, what your rights are and how to exercise them, as well as the security measures adopted.
controller:
The controller of your personal data processing is your employer, i.e., the company or organization with which you have an employment relationship and which uses the CheckIO platform for attendance and time tracking management.

CheckIO acts as a processor or data processing agent, providing technological services to your employer. CheckIO processes data only according to your employer's instructions and within the framework of the service agreement between them.
purposes:
Your personal data are processed for the following purposes:

• Work attendance record (required): Check-in and check-out marks, schedules, attendance reports and compliance with labor regulations. Legal basis: performance of the employment contract and legal obligations.

• Photo on check-in (optional): Capture of your image during marking to verify your identity. Legal basis: express consent.

• Fingerprint / biometry (optional): Capture and storage of biometric data for authentication during marking. Legal basis: express consent.

• Geolocation (optional): Recording of your location when marking to verify you are at the authorized place. Legal basis: express consent.

• Email communications (optional): Sending notifications about attendance, schedules and work matters. Legal basis: express consent.
dataCategories:
The categories of personal data processed include:

• Identification data: name, identification number, contact details.

• Employment data: schedules, attendance marks, shifts, permissions, absences.

• Biometric data (sensitive): fingerprint, when you have given your consent.

• Geolocation data: geographic coordinates at the time of marking, when you have given your consent.

• Images: photographs captured during marking, when you have given your consent.
legalBasis:
Processing is based on:

• Performance of the employment contract and compliance with legal obligations: for attendance record and everything related to mandatory time control.

• Express consent: for optional processing (photo, biometry, geolocation, email communications). You may revoke these consents at any time from the platform's privacy section.
retention:
• Attendance data: Retained for the periods established by applicable labor regulations in your country (generally 5 to 6 years after the end of the employment relationship).

• Biometric data: Retained while your consent remains active. After revocation, they are deleted within a reasonable technical period.

• Other optional data: Retained while your consent is active or for the period necessary to fulfill the purpose for which they were collected.
rights:
You have the right to:

• Access: Know what data of yours is being processed and obtain a copy.

• Rectification: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data.

• Erasure (right to be forgotten): Request deletion of your data when applicable.

• Objection: Object to processing in cases provided by law.

• Restriction of processing: Request that processing be restricted in certain circumstances.

• Data portability: Receive your data in a structured, commonly used format when processing is based on consent or contract.

These rights are personal, non-transferable and waivable.
rightsProcedure:
To exercise your rights, you must submit a written request to your employer's Human Resources department, to the email address or channel established by the company for this purpose.

Your employer will acknowledge receipt of the request and respond within the applicable period according to your country's regulations (e.g., in Chile: 30 calendar days, extendable by 30 more days; in Brazil: according to LGPD; in other countries: according to local legislation). The response will be sent to you in writing at your address or email.

In case of total or partial denial, you may file a complaint with the competent data protection authority in your country, within the period established by law (e.g., in Chile: 30 business days before the Personal Data Protection Agency).
transfers: When data is processed or stored outside the territory of your country (e.g., in cloud infrastructure in other states), your employer and CheckIO guarantee that adequate measures are adopted, such as: standard contractual clauses, compliance certifications or verification that the destination country offers an adequate level of protection, in accordance with the applicable regulations in each jurisdiction.
security:
Appropriate technical and organizational measures have been implemented to protect your personal data, including:

• Encryption of sensitive data (especially biometrics).

• Access control (only authorized personnel).

• Duty of confidentiality for persons involved in processing.

• Privacy by design and by default.

• Procedures to report and manage security incidents affecting your data.
contact: For any questions about the processing of your data or to exercise your rights, you must contact your employer's Human Resources department. Your employer is the data controller and must attend to your requests.
authoritiesIntro: You may file complaints with the competent authority in your country:

authorities.BR: Brazil: National Data Protection Authority - ANPD
authorities.AR: Argentina: National Directorate for the Protection of Personal Data
authorities.MX: Mexico: National Institute of Transparency, Access to Information and Personal Data Protection - INAI
authorities.CO: Colombia: Superintendency of Industry and Commerce - SIC
authorities.PE: Peru: National Authority for the Protection of Personal Data - ANPDP
authorities.UY: Uruguay: Personal Data Regulatory and Control Unit - URCDP
authorities.other: Other countries: Consult the data protection authority in your jurisdiction.
